23.reason: [The condition incorrectly checks s[i+1] <= 'z' etc., instead of checking both characters are within the same range; also, when i=0, s[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition incorrectly checks s[i+1] <= 'z' etc., instead of checking both characters are within the same range; also, when i=0, s[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition incorrectly checks ranges; e.g., 'a' to 'z' should use && for both bounds, but the logic is flawed and does not ensure same type. Also, when i=0, s[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Incorrect logic for checking same character type; does not verify both characters are within the same range, and accesses s[i-1] when i=0 causing out-of-bounds.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: condition does not verify both characters are of the same type (e.g., 'a-9' incorrectly passes). Also, accessing s[i-1] when i=0 causes out-of-bounds memory access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect range checks: conditions like 's[i-1]>='a' && s[i+1]<='z'' do not ensure both characters are of the same type or within valid ranges; also accesses s[i-1] when i=0 which is out-of-bounds]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect range checks: conditions like 's[i-1]>='a' && s[i+1]<='z'' do not ensure both characters are of the same type or within valid ranges; also accesses s[i-1] when i=0 leads to out-of-bounds read]

25.reason: // Compilation error: C90 does not allow variable declaration in for loop initialization; use C99 or declare j before the loop.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop does not output the first and last characters of the expansion range, missing the start and end characters]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  // Compilation error: C90 does not allow variable declaration in for loop initialization; use C99 or declare j before the loop.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Loop condition j<s[i+1] excludes the end character of the range; should be j<=s[i+1] to fully expand it.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Relies on the flawed condition above and lacks boundary checks for i=0 or i=n-1, leading to out-of-bounds access and incorrect expansion.
